measure.note: MEASURE (post-run, out-of-band, non-blocking; folds into optimize/substrate/measure.w0-pedagogy.json for TRIAGE, parallel to the run's own checks.post). REPLACES the 5 former `regex-present "<field>:\s*\S"` header-presence ops (2026-07-09 hardening) — those were GAMEABLE: `\s*\S` with no per-line anchor is satisfied by an empty/TODO/placeholder value (a header with content "TODO" or a blank line that bleeds into the NEXT field's label both satisfied the old regex; proven via CHECK_KINDS['regex-present'] directly, independent of any run). This script asserts REAL, non-empty, non-placeholder content per field (lesson kind + every discovery/focal/reinforcement occurrence; `stage` validated against its concrete|represent|symbolize enum), line-anchored so one field's absence can no longer read as another's presence. It also derives the artifact path IN-SCRIPT from `<run>/.pi/run.json`'s persisted `artifacts[].path` rather than resolving the arg-lessonId template token in this op's own path — every run sampled in this product repo (through 2026-07-07) has NO persisted args block, so the OLD ops' arg-lessonId-keyed paths were silently dropped into `ops.rejected` on every real run (never evaluated at all); this op's own args is just the resolved RUN dir, which always resolves. Fail-closed throughout: a missing run.json/artifact/unreadable file is a nonzero-exit failure, never a silent pass. NOTE ON THIS PROSE: never write a literal double-brace arg-lessonId token in this note field — `resolveDeep` walks EVERY string in the op tree, including `note`, so a literal token here throws MissingArgError exactly like the bug it describes (caught during authoring; see measures.md §4).

REWIRED 2026-07-09 (closing the residual gap this op's own history named): was a `gate{kind:regex-absent}` whose `path` resolved the arg-lessonId template token directly — every run.json sampled in this repo (through 2026-07-09) has NO persisted args block, so `resolveDeep` threw MissingArgError and the WHOLE op silently dropped into `ops.rejected`, never evaluated at all (same failure class as `pedagogy-field-content-valid` before its own 2026-07-09 rewrite). Now a thin `run` op (mirrors nodes/w4a-composer + nodes/w2c-sound-design): the script derives the pedagogy.md path IN-SCRIPT from `<run>/.pi/run.json`'s persisted `nodes.w0-pedagogy.artifacts[]` (always written) instead of a template arg, so this op's own args is just `{{RUN}}`, which always resolves. Same substantive predicate as before ('audit checklist'|'self-check'|'self-audit'|✓|checkbox), now a real case-insensitive regex instead of hand-rolled character classes. VALIDATED against a real known-bad artifact: `_prior-runs/kptest-fenyuhe-six/pedagogy.PRE-FIX.md` carries a literal '## Audit checklist' section (9 numbered self-audit items) baked into the artifact — the script FAILS closed on that file and PASSES on the real fixed gold (`remotion-svg-primitives/lesson-data/kptest-count-to-two/pedagogy.md`). Catches prompt.md's own named anti-pattern: 'Put ALL self-audit / self-check / checklist-mark prose in your structured return, NEVER as prose sections inside the artifact.' See scripts/measure/check-self-audit-leakage.mjs for the full rationale. NOTE ON THIS PROSE: never write the literal double-brace arg-lessonId token in this note field — `resolveDeep` walks EVERY string in the op tree, including `note`, so a literal token here throws MissingArgError exactly like the bug it describes.
